21.correct: Among the peninsular rivers, the Narmada and Tapti flow westward into the Arabian Sea (through rift valleys).

wrong.A: The Krishna and Godavari flow east into the Bay of Bengal.
wrong.C: The Mahanadi and Subarnarekha flow east.
wrong.D: The Kaveri and Periyar - the Kaveri flows east; Periyar is a small west-flowing river, but the standard pair is Narmada and Tapti.

21.fact: Most peninsular rivers flow east, except the Narmada and Tapti which flow west.

22.correct: After the decline of the Gupta Empire, the Pushyabhuti dynasty (of Harshavardhana) rose to prominence in North India.

wrong.A: The Cholas ruled the far south.
wrong.B: The Vakatakas were earlier contemporaries of the Guptas in the Deccan.
wrong.D: The Cheras ruled in the south (Kerala region).

22.fact: Harshavardhana of the Pushyabhuti dynasty made Kannauj a great centre of power.

23.correct: The decline in India's Infant Mortality Rate is due to all of these - better newborn care, expanded immunisation and improved maternal health.

wrong.A: Better newborn care is one factor but not the only one.
wrong.B: Immunisation is one factor among several.
wrong.C: Improved maternal health is one of several factors.

23.fact: India's IMR has fallen steadily, though it still varies widely across states.

24.correct: The Indian Independence Act, 1947 provided the legal basis for the creation of Pakistan and the partition of India.

wrong.A: The Cabinet Mission Plan (1946) proposed a united India framework.
wrong.C: The Government of India Act, 1935 was a colonial constitutional act.
wrong.D: The Mountbatten Plan was the proposal; the legal basis was the 1947 Act.

24.fact: The Act created the two Dominions of India and Pakistan on 15 August 1947.

25.correct: On Independence Day 2025, a 'Demography Mission' was announced to tackle illegal immigration.

wrong.B: 'Bharat Suraksha Abhiyan' was not the announced mission.
wrong.C: 'National Unity and Integrity Mission' was not announced.
wrong.D: 'Population Stabilisation Mission' was not the one announced.

25.fact: Demographic changes in border areas are a sensitive policy issue in India.

26.correct: The government set up the Malhotra Committee (chaired by former RBI Governor R.N. Malhotra) in 1993 to recommend insurance-sector reforms.

wrong.D: 1999 was when the IRDA Act was passed, following the committee's recommendations.

26.fact: The Malhotra Committee's report led to the opening up of the insurance sector and the creation of IRDA.

27.correct: 'Job security' (I) is NOT a pillar of the DAJGUA (Dharti Aaba) tribal-development campaign, so 'Only I' is the correct choice (the one that is not a pillar).

wrong.B: 'Only II' is wrong; cultural inclusion-type aims are closer to the campaign's pillars.

27.fact: The Dharti Aaba campaign focuses on saturating tribal villages with basic services and infrastructure.

28.correct: 'Dashavatar' is a traditional folk theatre/dance form of Goa (and the Konkan region).

wrong.D: It is not from Himachal Pradesh.

28.fact: Dashavatar depicts the ten incarnations (avatars) of Lord Vishnu.

29.correct: In October 2024, Cyclone 'Dana' affected Odisha and West Bengal.

wrong.D: Cyclone Shaheen affected the Arabian Sea region.

29.fact: Tropical cyclones in the Bay of Bengal frequently affect the Odisha-Bengal coast.

30.correct: The 'Harballabh Sangeet Mela', one of India's oldest classical music festivals, is held in Jalandhar (Punjab).

wrong.A: It is not held in Gwalior.
wrong.B: It is not held in Bhopal.
wrong.D: It is not held in Varanasi.

30.fact: The Harballabh festival has been held for over 145 years in Jalandhar.

31.correct: When the temperature of a substance rises, the kinetic energy of its particles increases (they move faster).

wrong.A: It does not decrease with rising temperature.
wrong.C: It does not stay constant when temperature rises.
wrong.D: It is not converted into potential energy by heating.

31.fact: Temperature is a measure of the average kinetic energy of the particles of a substance.

32.correct: When tax collection exceeds necessary expenditure, the budget is called a surplus budget.

wrong.A: A deficit budget is when expenditure exceeds revenue.
wrong.C: A balanced budget is when revenue equals expenditure.
wrong.D: A zero-based budget is a budgeting method, not this situation.

32.fact: Governments more commonly run deficit budgets to fund development spending.

33.correct: In the context of mineral resources in India, 'CBM' stands for Coal Bed Methane.

wrong.B: It does not mean Copper Bearing Mineral.
wrong.C: It does not mean Crude Biofuel Mixture.
wrong.D: It does not mean Compressed Bauxite Material.

33.fact: Coal Bed Methane is natural gas extracted from coal seams, an unconventional energy source.

34.correct: The first World Wrestling Championship was held in 1904.
34.fact: Wrestling is one of the oldest sports, included in the ancient Olympic Games.

35.correct: Both Bombay (Mumbai) and Pune were host cities of the 1994 National Games (held in Maharashtra), so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both hosted events.

35.fact: The National Games are held across multiple cities of the host state.

36.correct: In 1993, India's share of world service exports was only about 0.5%.

wrong.C: 8% is far too high for that period.

36.fact: India's services exports (especially IT) grew dramatically after the 1991 reforms.

37.correct: In tennis, when both players reach 40-40 (three points each), the score is called 'deuce'.

wrong.C: A 'slice' is a type of shot.
wrong.D: A 'smash' is an overhead attacking shot.

37.fact: From deuce, a player must win two consecutive points (advantage, then game) to win.

38.correct: Bhutan, a Himalayan nation, has been exploring the mining of 'green' cryptocurrency using its abundant hydropower.

wrong.B: Nepal is not the country described.
wrong.C: Pakistan is not associated with this.
wrong.D: India is not the Himalayan nation referred to here.

38.fact: Bhutan has used its surplus clean hydroelectricity for digital-asset mining.

39.correct: In India, the 'RESIDEX' index relates to housing prices (residential property price trends).

wrong.A: It does not track stock-market performance.
wrong.B: It does not track industrial production.
wrong.C: It does not track agricultural commodities.

39.fact: NHB RESIDEX is India's official housing price index, published by the National Housing Bank.

40.correct: The Global Terrorism Index is released by the Institute for Economics and Peace, which is based in Australia.

wrong.B: It is not based in the USA.
wrong.C: It is not based in Germany.
wrong.D: It is not based in Sweden.

40.fact: The Institute for Economics and Peace also produces the Global Peace Index.
